page: Microsoft Azure L64as_v4 is a high-capacity server instance belonging to the Lasv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. This virtual machine features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage-heavy workloads, the instance includes 15359 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor under the x86_64 architecture and does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core and data processing capabilities. The server achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 21342.0, placing it in the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reached 366.38 GB/sec, and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance registered at 98427.49, both ranking in the top 25%. Database and transactional performance are also notable, with Passmark database operations scoring 27733.64 and Redis SET operations executing at 89.18 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth tests show strong performance for cached small-block reads at 4861.79 GB/sec and large-block writes at 106.62 GB/sec, though uncached large-block reads perform at an average rate of 150.96 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L64as_v4 offers a high density of local storage and memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for deployments requiring substantial local data caching without the overhead of external storage arrays. The absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, but it demonstrates strong capability in CPU-based LLM inference, processing 3999.55 tokens per second on small models. This hardware profile is highly suited for large-scale databases, transactional systems, data warehousing, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure L64as_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized virtual machine from the Lasv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 512.0 GB of RAM, alongside a substantial 15359 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21342.0 and Redis SET performance of 89.18 million operations per second. While it lacks GPU acceleration, its high storage capacity and memory density provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive applications. The L64as_v4 is highly suited for large-scale databases, transactional workloads, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L64as_v4 is a Lasv4-family virtual machine featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 15359 GB of local storage, optimized for large-scale database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L64as_v4 features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 15.3 TB of local storage, optimized for database and transactional workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity AMD EPYC instance featuring substantial local storage and memory density designed for demanding database and transactional workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 512 GB of system memory and 15359 GB of local storage
Strong transactional performance with 89.18 million Redis SET operations per second
Top-tier multi-core performance with a Geekbench score of 21342.0
Best suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and transactional workloads
